title: Paystack keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Paystack test secret key detected in source code. Secret keys grant full access to your Paystack account including initiation of payments, transfers, refunds, and access to customer data. Exposing these in code allows attackers to process fraudulent transactions and access sensitive payment information. Store securely using environment variables or secret management systems. This is a test key but should still be protected.

Paystack LIVE secret key detected in source code. LIVE secret keys provide full programmatic access to production Paystack accounts including payment processing, customer data, transfers, and refunds. Exposing these allows attackers to initiate fraudulent financial transactions, steal customer payment information, and cause significant financial damage. IMMEDIATELY rotate this key and store securely using environment variables or secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault).

Paystack test public key detected in source code. While public keys are less sensitive than secret keys, they should still be managed carefully as they identify your Paystack account and can be used in client-side payment flows. Test keys are for development environments only. Store in configuration files or environment variables.

Paystack LIVE public key detected in source code. While public keys are intended for client-side use, exposing them in source code repositories can reveal production Paystack account details and enable attackers to identify active payment integrations. LIVE keys are for production environments only. Store in configuration files or environment variables and implement proper access controls.
